steps.title: Interpret the strength comparison
steps.content: The researchers concluded “correlations with velocity are stronger than those with density”—meaning velocity correlations must have greater absolute distance from 0 than density correlations.

steps.title: Match the claim to the data
steps.content: Choice D states that for each rating, velocity correlations are further from 0 than density correlations. The data confirms this across all three: Before (|−0.9357| > |0.8592|), After (|−0.9518| > |0.7308|), Overall (|−0.8587| > |0.7447|). The other choices either don't compare velocity to density systematically, or describe the sign pattern rather than the relative strength of correlations. That matches choice D — D is correct.
